notes: Carrying a baton in India may be legal in some contexts, but carrying it as a weapon can lead to legal issues.
Kubotans are generally legal in India as simple keychain tools
Self-defence keychains are generally legal in India if they are ordinary items and not clearly designed as weapons.
Carrying a sword in public in India may be legal in certain cultural or ceremonial contexts but can be restricted otherwise.
Tasers are not clearly regulated in India but may be restricted under broader weapons laws, making civilian ownership risky.
Pepper spray is generally legal for personal self-defence in India
Switchblades are regulated in India, with legality depending on blade size and local laws.
Butterfly knives are not specifically banned but may be restricted depending on blade size and intended use.
Stun guns fall into a grey area in India and may be restricted under arms laws depending on classification and use.
Brass knuckles are generally considered illegal weapons in India and can lead to criminal charges if possessed.
